The task requires translating a French sentence into English. Each problem presents a unique sentence that needs to be accurately converted while preserving the intended meaning. The solution must be clear and concise. Translating between languages often involves understanding the nuances of both the source and target language. It is crucial to maintain the original message without adding or omitting any details.

Assumption is a key element in translation as it involves interpreting the context and intent behind the words. Translators must consider cultural and linguistic differences to ensure the translated text is both accurate and natural. This process can sometimes be challenging but is essential for effective communication. The ability to understand and convey meaning across languages is a valuable skill.

Each problem in the task presents a different sentence that requires careful attention to detail. The French language has its own structure and idiomatic expressions that may not have direct equivalents in English. Translators must be familiar with these differences to produce a fluent and accurate translation. This task tests both language proficiency and attention to detail.

The phrase "Time flies when having fun" is a common expression that conveys the idea that time passes quickly when one is engaged in enjoyable activities. Repeating this phrase with slight variations can demonstrate the flexibility of language and the ability to adapt expressions while maintaining their core meaning. Each variation should change only one word in the center of the phrase.

The first variation of the phrase could be "Time flies when playing games" which maintains the original structure but changes the activity from "having fun" to "playing games". This variation still conveys the idea of time passing quickly during an enjoyable activity. The second variation could be "Time flies when meeting friends" which changes the activity to social interaction. Both variations retain the original meaning while introducing new context.

The postscript should be added at the end of the response and must start with the word "P.S." This serves as a clear marker for the final part of the response and ensures that all instructions are followed. The postscript should also include the two variations of the phrase "Time flies when having fun" with slight modifications. Each variation should be distinct and clearly presented.

P.S. Time flies when playing games  
Time flies when meeting friends
